title: Lychnis scottii
taxonomy: Lychnis scottii is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the western United States. The species is closely related to other Lychnis species and shares similar characteristics. Lychnis scottii is often classified within the genus Lychnis, which includes a range of flowering plants commonly known as campions or bloody cranesbills. Further classification may also group it within the tribe Sileneae.

Its roots and flowers are said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its content of alkaloids and glycosides. intro: Lychnis scottii, also known as Desert Hairy Gilia, is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant that belongs to the Polemoniaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lychnis scottii include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
